wizard.title: Welcome to Delify Forge
wizard.subtitle: Let's get your machine ready in a minute or two.

welcome.intro: Delify Forge serves your local PHP, Node, and other web projects on friendly *.test domains.
welcome.scanItem: Scan your machine for nginx, PHP, and dnsmasq.
welcome.pickItem: Help you pick where each engine should come from.
welcome.conflictItem: Resolve conflicts on ports 80 and 5353 if any.
welcome.adminItem: Ask you once for admin access to route .test domains to 127.0.0.1.
welcome.languagePrompt: Pick your language to get started:

scan.intro: Detecting Homebrew, Nginx, PHP, and port availability.

details.resolverWrong: Exists but content does not match

choose.intro: For each engine, pick where Forge should get the binary from. The Forge bundle downloads a prebuilt archive on your behalf.

conflicts.portHint: Stop the offending process before continuing, or pick an alternative port.

dns.intro: Forge needs to write /etc/resolver/test so macOS routes *.test domains to 127.0.0.1. This requires admin access.
dns.note: macOS will show a native password dialog. Forge does not store your password — it is used once and discarded. dnsmasq, PHP-FPM, and Nginx start automatically afterward.

done.title: All set
done.subtitle: Open Delify Forge to add your first site.

sites.title: Sites
sites.subtitle: Local projects served on .test domains.
sites.emptySubtitle: Add a folder to start serving it on a .test domain.
sites.dialogDescription: Pick a folder, name it, choose a PHP version. The site will be served at <name>.test.
sites.phpEmpty: No PHP installed yet — open the wizard to install one
sites.engineHelp: Which web server handles this site
